commit.message:
chore(release): v0.55.0 (#11392)

Automated final release preparation for v0.55.0.

## Contributor checklist

Please verify that the PR meets the following requirements, where
applicable:

<!--
This checklist uses "TaskRadio" comments to make certain options
mutually exclusive.
See:
https://github.com/mheap/require-checklist-action?tab=readme-ov-file#radio-groups
For details on how this works and why it's required.
-->

- An overview of proposed schema changes is included in a linked GitHub
issue.
    - [ ] Yes <!-- TaskRadio schema -->
    - [x] Not applicable <!-- TaskRadio schema -->
- A design document PR is created in the [design-notes
repository](https://github.com/radius-project/design-notes/), if new
APIs are being introduced.
    - [ ] Yes <!-- TaskRadio design-pr -->
    - [x] Not applicable <!-- TaskRadio design-pr -->
- The design document has been reviewed and approved by Radius
maintainers/approvers.
    - [ ] Yes <!-- TaskRadio design-review -->
    - [x] Not applicable <!-- TaskRadio design-review -->
- A PR for the [samples
repository](https://github.com/radius-project/samples) is created, if
existing samples are affected by the changes in this PR.
    - [ ] Yes <!-- TaskRadio samples-pr -->
    - [x] Not applicable <!-- TaskRadio samples-pr -->
- A PR for the [documentation
repository](https://github.com/radius-project/docs) is created, if the
changes in this PR affect the documentation or any user facing updates
are made.
    - [ ] Yes <!-- TaskRadio docs-pr -->
    - [x] Not applicable <!-- TaskRadio docs-pr -->
- A PR for the [recipes
repository](https://github.com/radius-project/recipes) is created, if
existing recipes are affected by the changes in this PR.
    - [ ] Yes <!-- TaskRadio recipes-pr -->
    - [x] Not applicable <!-- TaskRadio recipes-pr -->

---------

Signed-off-by: Dariusz Porowski <3431813+DariuszPorowski@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(water): stencil-based shoreline clipping

- terrain.frag: discard vHeight<0 → kein Stencil unter Wasser
- WorldManager: terrain-material schreibt stencil=1 (nur Land-Pixel)
- WaterManager: wasser rendert nur wo stencil=0 (Ozean)
  Plane von 30km auf 12km reduziert (Nebel bedeckt den Rand)

Effekt: Wasser endet exakt an der Küste, geht nicht
unter die Insel. Höhlen und Krater werden korrekt
abgeschirmt sobald sie implementiert werden.

commit.message:
[WIP] Fix persistent dirty worktree failure in perplexity_models.yml (#349)

* Initial plan

* fix: stop writing tracked debug files after commit/before rebase

Replace `tee debug/pre_rebase_status.txt` and `tee debug/post_commit_dirty_worktree.txt`
with in-memory shell variables. The cleanliness gate was creating the very condition it
tested for: `tee` truncated the tracked file, causing `git status` to report it as
modified.

Also remove both files from git tracking and add them to .gitignore.

Co-authored-by: goldbondman <4745804+goldbondman@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use here-string for dirty file count to avoid echo edge case

Co-authored-by: goldbondman <4745804+goldbondman@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Remove dead “move block to new window” path and dependent unused APIs (#3002)

`WindowService.MoveBlockToNewWindow` appears to be unreferenced, and its
supporting path had become isolated. This change removes that dead RPC
surface and the backend/eventbus helpers that existed only for that
flow.

- **Window service cleanup (backend RPC)**
- Removed `MoveBlockToNewWindow_Meta` and `MoveBlockToNewWindow` from
`pkg/service/windowservice/windowservice.go`.
  - Dropped now-unused imports tied to that method (`log`, `eventbus`).

- **Store cleanup**
  - Removed `MoveBlockToTab` from `pkg/wstore/wstore.go`.
  - Removed now-unused `utilfn` import from the same file.

- **Eventbus cleanup**
  - Removed unused event constant `WSEvent_ElectronNewWindow`.
- Removed `getWindowWatchesForWindowId` and `BusyWaitForWindowId`, which
were only used by the deleted move-to-new-window path.
  - Removed now-unused `time` import.

- **Generated frontend service surface**
- Regenerated service bindings and removed
`WindowServiceType.MoveBlockToNewWindow(...)` from
`frontend/app/store/services.ts`.

Example of removed RPC surface:
```ts
// removed from frontend/app/store/services.ts
MoveBlockToNewWindow(currentTabId: string, blockId: string): Promise<void> {
    return WOS.callBackendService("window", "MoveBlockToNewWindow", Array.from(arguments))
}
```

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT TIPS -->
---

✨ Let Copilot coding agent [set things up for
you](https://github.com/wavetermdev/waveterm/issues/new?title=✨+Set+up+Copilot+instructions&body=Configure%20instructions%20for%20this%20repository%20as%20documented%20in%20%5BBest%20practices%20for%20Copilot%20coding%20agent%20in%20your%20repository%5D%28https://gh.io/copilot-coding-agent-tips%29%2E%0A%0A%3COnboard%20this%20repo%3E&assignees=copilot)
— coding agent works faster and does higher quality work when set up for
your repo.

---------

fix: resolve missing logger, add return type hints, fix JSON regex

  'logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)' — previously crashed at runtime
  when a database commit failed (NameError on logger.error call)
- backend/database.py: add Generator[Session, None, None] return type to
  get_db() and -> None to init_db(); import Generator from typing
- backend/main.py: add AsyncGenerator import; annotate lifespan(),
  root(), get_cluster_status(), and spa_catch_all() with return types
- ai-engine/diagnoser.py: change non-greedy .*? to greedy .* in the
  markdown-fence JSON regex so nested JSON objects are fully captured;
  add -> None to AIDiagnoser.__init__()
- ai-engine/analyzers/{openai,gemini,ollama}_analyzer.py: add -> None
  return type to all __init__ methods

All 45 tests pass.

commit.message:
Friday Staging to Main Merge (#82)

* fix: correct template expression spacing in secret availability check

Add missing space after opening braces in secrets.GH_APP_ID reference
for consistent YAML parsing across both secret checks.

* Initial plan

* fix: revert cla.yml job id from cla back to cla_assistant

Co-authored-by: jenperret <237345776+jenperret@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: retrigger checks for PR #56

* Revise governance layer description in README

Updated the description of the governance layer to emphasize the need for an agent team that aligns with team trust and compliance requirements.

* fix: Update CLA workflow to use GH_CE_* naming convention for staging

- Replace GH_APP_ID with GH_CE_APP_ID (2 occurrences)
- Replace GH_APP_PRIVATE_KEY with GH_CE_APP_PRIVATE_KEY (2 occurrences)
- Ensures naming consistency with staging branch deployment standard
- Maintains Community Edition secret naming convention across all workflows

* fix: add GitHub App secret availability check to CLA workflow (#76)

* fix: correct template expression spacing in secret availability check

Add missing space after opening braces in secrets.GH_APP_ID reference
for consistent YAML parsing across both secret checks.

* Initial plan

* fix: revert cla.yml job id from cla back to cla_assistant

Co-authored-by: jenperret <237345776+jenperret@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: retrigger checks for PR #56

* fix: Update CLA workflow to use GH_CE_* naming convention for staging

- Replace GH_APP_ID with GH_CE_APP_ID (2 occurrences)
- Replace GH_APP_PRIVATE_KEY with GH_CE_APP_PRIVATE_KEY (2 occurrences)
- Ensures naming consistency with staging branch deployment standard
- Maintains Community Edition secret naming convention across all workflows

---------

Co-authored-by: AICraftWorks <jenp@outlook.com>
Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: jenperret <237345776+jenperret@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: only deploy staging on push, not on PR events

- Remove pull_request trigger to prevent environment protection errors
- PRs will build/test only; deploy happens after merge to staging branch
- Simplifies workflow by removing merged PR condition check
- Resolves 'refs/pull/*/merge not allowed to deploy' errors

Fixes #76 environment protection issue

* fix: remove branch naming requirement for PRs into staging

- Only enforce 'staging -> main' promotion rule
- Allow any branch name to create PRs into staging
- Keeps main protected (only staging can merge to main)
- Resolves PR #77 branch policy validation failures

This allows patches like 'Jenp-AICraftWorks-patch-1' to merge to staging.

Fixes: https://github.com/AgentCraftworks/AgentCraftworks-CE/actions/runs/22786580070

* Revise governance layer description in README (#77)

Updated the description of the governance layer to emphasize the need for an agent team that aligns with team trust and compliance requirements.

Co-authored-by: AICraftWorks <jenp@outlook.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: AICraftWorks <jenp@outlook.com>

commit.message:
fix: fall back to globalType.defaultDayRate in resource profile cost calculation

Project-level resource types inherit day rates from global types, but the
resource profile query only fetched resourceTypes: true (no globalType join).
When dayRate was null on the project ResourceType, hasCost stayed false and
cost columns were hidden even though global rates were set.

Fix: include globalType in both resourceTypes and overheads queries, and
resolve: resourceType.dayRate ?? resourceType.globalType?.defaultDayRate

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(world): update minimap, teleports and spawn to new terrain

- minimap.jpg: regenerated from Aethelgard_norm.npy with color coding
  (water=blue, sand, grass, forest, rock, snow by height)
- Teleports updated to actual island locations:
  Goldstrand (73237,79616), Tramuntana (64898,26584),
  Aethelhafen (58944,56760), Nordkueste (93872,6327)
- Spawn: (58799,54589) - flat area near island center (Chunk 24,22)
- Server WorldEntity + characters route updated

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Edit original messages instead of sending duplicates

- RatingRangeView edits its own message with the result
- SourceChoiceView edits its own message (Notion shows match results inline)
- No more duplicate confirmation messages

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix autocomplete: use fetch ID instead of query comparison for stale guard

The previous lastQuery check was too aggressive — every keystroke updated
lastQuery, causing debounced responses to be discarded as 'stale' even
when they were the most recent fetch. This made autocomplete appear to
wait until typing stopped, and Nominatim results (Barclays Center, etc.)
were silently dropped.

Now uses an incrementing fetchId that only changes when a new fetch
actually fires after debounce, so results are shown promptly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf(tests): optimize Api.Tests.Integration — shared fixture, parallel collections

- Replace 23 per-class MongoDbContainers with ICollectionFixture<MongoDbFixture>
- Split single 'Integration' collection into 4 parallel domain collections:
  CategoryIntegration, IssueIntegration, CommentIntegration, StatusIntegration
- Enable parallelizeTestCollections: true in xunit.runner.json
- Use unique DB name per test class instance (Guid-based) for full isolation
- Reduce large dataset test from 1000 to 100 issues; add [Trait('Category','slow')]
- Expected speedup: 5-10 min -> ~2-3 min (23 container startups -> 4 parallel)

commit.message:
Fix/deploy staging no pr trigger (#81)

* fix: correct template expression spacing in secret availability check

Add missing space after opening braces in secrets.GH_APP_ID reference
for consistent YAML parsing across both secret checks.

* Initial plan

* fix: revert cla.yml job id from cla back to cla_assistant

Co-authored-by: jenperret <237345776+jenperret@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: retrigger checks for PR #56

* Revise governance layer description in README

Updated the description of the governance layer to emphasize the need for an agent team that aligns with team trust and compliance requirements.

* fix: Update CLA workflow to use GH_CE_* naming convention for staging

- Replace GH_APP_ID with GH_CE_APP_ID (2 occurrences)
- Replace GH_APP_PRIVATE_KEY with GH_CE_APP_PRIVATE_KEY (2 occurrences)
- Ensures naming consistency with staging branch deployment standard
- Maintains Community Edition secret naming convention across all workflows

* fix: only deploy staging on push, not on PR events

- Remove pull_request trigger to prevent environment protection errors
- PRs will build/test only; deploy happens after merge to staging branch
- Simplifies workflow by removing merged PR condition check
- Resolves 'refs/pull/*/merge not allowed to deploy' errors

Fixes #76 environment protection issue

* fix: remove branch naming requirement for PRs into staging

- Only enforce 'staging -> main' promotion rule
- Allow any branch name to create PRs into staging
- Keeps main protected (only staging can merge to main)
- Resolves PR #77 branch policy validation failures

Fixes: https://github.com/AgentCraftworks/AgentCraftworks-CE/actions/runs/22786580070

---------

Co-authored-by: AICraftWorks <jenp@outlook.com>
Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: jenperret <237345776+jenperret@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] Prepare release 8.3.0 (#462)

🤖 *This PR was created by Repo Assist, an automated AI assistant.*

Prepares the RELEASE_NOTES.md for version **8.3.0** — a minor release
capturing significant improvements merged since 8.2.0 (February 24).

## Changes since 8.2.0

| Type | PR | Description |
|------|----|-------------|
| 🐛 Bug fix | #432 | Fix custom attributes on nested erased types |
| 🐛 Bug fix | #458 | Fix `GetNestedType` on
`TypeSymbol`/`ProvidedTypeSymbol` for generic provided types |
| 🐛 Bug fix | #459 | Fix mutable variable captures in
`QuotationSimplifier` — promote to ref cells |
| ⚡ Performance | #443 | Memoize `transType` in `AssemblyCompiler` to
reduce redundant type translation |
| ⚡ Performance | #457 | Cache `transTypeRef` and `transMethRef` in
assembly compiler |
| ✨ Feature | #428 | New warning when all static parameters in a type
provider are optional |
| 📚 Docs | #455 | Documentation guide overhaul |
| 🧪 Tests | #442 | Add coverage tests and Coverage build target |
| 🔧 Toolchain | #431 | Update to .NET 8 SDK and toolchain |

This is a minor version bump (8.2.0 → 8.3.0) due to the new feature
(#428) and significant improvements. If preferred, a patch release
(8.2.1) is also reasonable given the emphasis on bug fixes.

## Test Status

This PR only modifies RELEASE_NOTES.md. The build/test status for the
underlying changes is tracked in the individual PRs listed above (all
passed CI before merging).

---

*To release: merge this PR, then tag `v8.3.0` and publish the NuGet
package via the existing build pipeline.*
